syntax_9329.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_9329.title: Integer Range
syntax_9329.description:
Represents a range between 2 integers.
Use the IntegerRange expression to get the high/low value.

en:
Represents a range between 2 integers.
Use the IntegerRange expression to get the high/low value.

syntax_9327.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_9327.title: IntegerRange - Low/High
syntax_9327.description: Get the low/high point of an integer range.

en: Get the low/high point of an integer range.

syntax_9326.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_9326.title: Brig Command Arg
syntax_9326.description:
Represents the arguments in a Brig Command. These works the same way as Skript's `arg` and `arg-1`.
Since command args create local variables at runtime, these are virtually useless.
These can be used in both the `register argument` and `trigger` sections.

en:
Represents the arguments in a Brig Command. These works the same way as Skript's `arg` and `arg-1`.
Since command args create local variables at runtime, these are virtually useless.
These can be used in both the `register argument` and `trigger` sections.

syntax_9388.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_9388.title: Apply Suggestion
syntax_9388.description:
Apply a suggestion with tooltip or list of suggestions to an argument.
This is used only in the argument registration section of a brig command and the suggestions section of a big command tree.
syntax_9388.syntax_pattern:
apply suggestion %string% with tooltip %~object%
apply suggestion[s] %~objects%

en:
Apply a suggestion with tooltip or list of suggestions to an argument.
This is used only in the argument registration section of a brig command and the suggestions section of a big command tree.

syntax_9799.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_9799.title: Particle Data
syntax_9799.description:
Represents a particle along with its provided data.
This is retrieved when using the `particle` argument.
You can get the particle/data from this type using the appropriate expressions.

en:
Represents a particle along with its provided data.
This is retrieved when using the `particle` argument.
You can get the particle/data from this type using the appropriate expressions.

syntax_9797.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_9797.title: ParticleData - Data Type
syntax_9797.description: Represents the data type of a ParticleData.

en: Represents the data type of a ParticleData.

syntax_9798.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_9798.title: ParticleData - Particle
syntax_9798.description: Represents the particle of a ParticleData.

en: Represents the particle of a ParticleData.

syntax_11446.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_11446.title: Predicate - Matches
syntax_11446.description: Check if a block/item matches a predicate.

en: Check if a block/item matches a predicate.

syntax_11447.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_11447.title: Predicate - Filter
syntax_11447.description: Represents a item/block predicate for filtering objects.
syntax_11447.syntax_pattern:
%itemstacks% (that|which) match item[[ ]stack] predicate %predicate%
%blocks% (that|which) match block predicate %predicate%

en: Represents a item/block predicate for filtering objects.

syntax_11448.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_11448.title: Predicate
syntax_11448.description: Represents a predicate which can be used for filtering.

en: Represents a predicate which can be used for filtering.

syntax_11856.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_11856.title: CommandTree - SubCommand
syntax_11856.description:
Register a sub command in a command tree.
A sub command is just an argument that can have its own sub commands and triggers.
See [**SkBriggy Wiki**](https://github.com/ShaneBeee/SkBriggy/wiki/Command-Tree) for more detailed info.
**Notes**:
- A `greedy string` arg always has to be last, you cannot register another subcommand within it.
- Optionals are a little funny, you cannot have a required subcommand within an optional subcommand.
- Min/Max can only be used on number subcommands.
- The name/id you choose for your subcommand will automatically be made into a local variable.
- List arg types (ie: players/entities) will create list variables. DO NOT repeat names. See examples.

**Entries/Sections**:
`permission` = Each subcommand can have its own permission.
`suggestions` = You can apply suggestions (with tooltips) to a subcommand. See `apply suggestion` effect, and examples.
`register arg` = Register another subcommand within this one. Supports multiple.
`trigger` = Like any other command, this is what will execute when the command is run.
syntax_11856.entries: [{"name": "permission", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "suggestions", "isRequired": false, "isSection": true}, {"name": "register arg section", "isRequired": false, "isSection": true}, {"name": "trigger", "isRequired": false, "isSection": true}]

en:
Register a sub command in a command tree.
A sub command is just an argument that can have its own sub commands and triggers.
See [**SkBriggy Wiki**](https://github.com/ShaneBeee/SkBriggy/wiki/Command-Tree) for more detailed info.
**Notes**:
- A `greedy string` arg always has to be last, you cannot register another subcommand within it.
- Optionals are a little funny, you cannot have a required subcommand within an optional subcommand.
- Min/Max can only be used on number subcommands.
- The name/id you choose for your subcommand will automatically be made into a local variable.
- List arg types (ie: players/entities) will create list variables. DO NOT repeat names. See examples.

**Entries/Sections**:
`permission` = Each subcommand can have its own permission.
`suggestions` = You can apply suggestions (with tooltips) to a subcommand. See `apply suggestion` effect, and examples.
`register arg` = Register another subcommand within this one. Supports multiple.
`trigger` = Like any other command, this is what will execute when the command is run.

syntax_11503.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_11503.title: Brig Command
syntax_11503.description:
Register a new Brigadier command.
See wiki for more details on registering: [**SkBriggy Wiki**](https://github.com/ShaneBeee/SkBriggy/wiki/Registering-New-Command)

Command Format:
`brig command /namespace:commandName <brigArgType> [<brigArgType>] <argName:brigArgType> [<argName:brigArgType>]:`
`namespace` = The namespace of a command. (Optional, defaults to "minecraft:")
`commandName` = Represents the command itself, ex: '/mycommand'.
`brigArgType` = Represents a brig argument type.
- While some may match Skript types, this doesn't actually support Skript types.
`argName` = The name of the arg, which will be used to create a local variable for the arg.
- In some cases this will show when typing out a command in game.
- If this isn't set a local variable will be created using the type (see examples).

Just like Skript commands, wrapping your arg in `[]` makes it optional. Do note at this time there is no support for defaults.

Entries and Sections:
`executor_type` = What types of execturs can run this command (Optional, defaults to `all`).
`permission:` = Just like Skript, the permission the player will require for this command.
`description:` = Just like Skript, this is a string that will be used in the help command.
`arguments:` = Section for registering arguments. See `Register Argument` effect.
`trigger:` = Section, just like Skript, for executing your code in the command.

Event Values:
`[event-]sender` = The sender (console/player/entity) who ran the command or the sender used in Minecraft's `/execute as <sender>` command.
`[event-]world` = World of the sender, or default world if sender is console, or world used in Minecraft's `/execute in <world>` command.
syntax_11503.entries: [{"name": "permission", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "description", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "executor_type", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "usage", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "aliases", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "arguments", "isRequired": false, "isSection": true}, {"name": "trigger", "isRequired": true, "isSection": true}]

en:
Register a new Brigadier command.
See wiki for more details on registering: [**SkBriggy Wiki**](https://github.com/ShaneBeee/SkBriggy/wiki/Registering-New-Command)

Command Format:
`brig command /namespace:commandName <brigArgType> [<brigArgType>] <argName:brigArgType> [<argName:brigArgType>]:`
`namespace` = The namespace of a command. (Optional, defaults to "minecraft:")
`commandName` = Represents the command itself, ex: '/mycommand'.
`brigArgType` = Represents a brig argument type.
- While some may match Skript types, this doesn't actually support Skript types.
`argName` = The name of the arg, which will be used to create a local variable for the arg.
- In some cases this will show when typing out a command in game.
- If this isn't set a local variable will be created using the type (see examples).

Just like Skript commands, wrapping your arg in `[]` makes it optional. Do note at this time there is no support for defaults.

Entries and Sections:
`executor_type` = What types of execturs can run this command (Optional, defaults to `all`).
`permission:` = Just like Skript, the permission the player will require for this command.
`description:` = Just like Skript, this is a string that will be used in the help command.
`arguments:` = Section for registering arguments. See `Register Argument` effect.
`trigger:` = Section, just like Skript, for executing your code in the command.

Event Values:
`[event-]sender` = The sender (console/player/entity) who ran the command or the sender used in Minecraft's `/execute as <sender>` command.
`[event-]world` = World of the sender, or default world if sender is console, or world used in Minecraft's `/execute in <world>` command.

syntax_9325.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_9325.title: Argument Suggestions
syntax_9325.description:
Set the suggestions for an argument.
Will accept any object, but strings are the best way to go.
For args you can use a number (the position of the argument) or a string (name of the argument), see examples.
NOTE: This expression is not dynamic, meaning if variables/expressions are used, they wont update.
syntax_9325.syntax_pattern:
suggestions of arg[ument][s] %strings/numbers%
suggestions of %strings/numbers% arg[ument][s]

en:
Set the suggestions for an argument.
Will accept any object, but strings are the best way to go.
For args you can use a number (the position of the argument) or a string (name of the argument), see examples.
NOTE: This expression is not dynamic, meaning if variables/expressions are used, they wont update.

syntax_14335.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14335.title: Executor Type
syntax_14335.description: Represents the different types that can run a command.
syntax_14335.type_usage: all, block, console, entity, feedback_forwarding, native, player, proxy, remote

en: Represents the different types that can run a command.

syntax_9389.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_9389.title: Register Argument
syntax_9389.description:
Register an argument for a brig command.

Within this section you can apply suggestions with tooltips.
The section itself will run when a player types a command, thus variables CAN be used and the [event-]player
will be the player typing the command.
Local variables will be created for the previously typed args, and can be used in this section. See examples.

`brigarg` = Type of argument. See Brig Argument Type for more details.

`string` = Name of the argument. (Used for local variables and how it shows in game)

`%objects`% = Suggestions for this argument. (If object is not a string, Skript will stringify it)

`min/max` = The min/max range of a number (long/int/float/double) argument.
syntax_9389.syntax_pattern:
register [optional] %*brigarg% arg[ument] [(named|with name)] %string%
register [optional] %*brigarg% arg[ument] [(named|with name)] %string% (with suggestions|using) %objects%
register [optional] %*brigarg% arg[ument] [(named|with name)] %string% with [min %-number%] [and] [with] [max %-number%]

en:
Register an argument for a brig command.

Within this section you can apply suggestions with tooltips.
The section itself will run when a player types a command, thus variables CAN be used and the [event-]player
will be the player typing the command.
Local variables will be created for the previously typed args, and can be used in this section. See examples.

`brigarg` = Type of argument. See Brig Argument Type for more details.

`string` = Name of the argument. (Used for local variables and how it shows in game)

`%objects`% = Suggestions for this argument. (If object is not a string, Skript will stringify it)

`min/max` = The min/max range of a number (long/int/float/double) argument.

syntax_9328.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_9328.title: Brig Argument Type
syntax_9328.description:
Represents a type of argument for a Brig Command.

All the types here represent object types in Minecraft, with built in conversions to return
Bukkit/Skript types to be able to fully use in Skript.

See Wiki for more details <link>https://github.com/ShaneBeee/SkBriggy/wiki/Brig-Argument-Type</link>
syntax_9328.type_usage: (namespaced[ ]key|mc[ ]key), angle, biome, biome[ ]key, block[ ]data, block[ ]pos, block[ ]predicate, block[ ]state, boolean, command, component, dimension, double, enchant[ment], entities, entity, entity[ ]data, entity[ ]type, float, game[ ]mode, greedy[ ]string, int[eger], int[eger][ ]range, item[ ]type, item[[ ]stack], item[[ ]stack][ ]predicate, lit[eral], loc[ation], loc[ation][ ]2d, long, loot[ ]table, message, nbt, objective, offline[ ]player, particle, player, players, potion[ ]effect[[ ]type], recipe, rotation, skript[ ]color, sound, string, team, text, time, time[ ]span, time[ ]span[ ]period, world

en:
Represents a type of argument for a Brig Command.

All the types here represent object types in Minecraft, with built in conversions to return
Bukkit/Skript types to be able to fully use in Skript.

See Wiki for more details <link>https://github.com/ShaneBeee/SkBriggy/wiki/Brig-Argument-Type</link>

syntax_11857.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_11857.title: CommandTree - Command
syntax_11857.description:
Command trees are similar to regular commands with the difference being the arguments are in a tree.
By having a tree, each argument can have sub args as well as their own triggers.
See [**SkBriggy Wiki**](https://github.com/ShaneBeee/SkBriggy/wiki/Command-Tree) for more detailed info.

Command names can include namespaces, ex: `brig command tree /mycommands:somecommand`.
Defaults to `minecraft` when excluded.

**Entries/Sections**:
`executor_type` = What types of execturs can run this command (Optional, defaults to `all`).
`permission` = Just like Skript, the permission the player will require for this command.
`description` = Just like Skript, this is a string that will be used in the help command.
`usages` = This is the usage which is shown in the specific `/help <command>` page. Separate multiple usages by comma.
`aliases` = Aliases for this command.
`override` = Whether to completely wipe out other commands with the same name, such as vanilla Minecraft commands (Defaults to false).
`register arg` = Register another subcommand within this one. Supports multiple.
`trigger` = Like any other command, this is what will execute when the command is run.
syntax_11857.entries: [{"name": "permission", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "description", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "override", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "executor_type", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "usages", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "aliases", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "register arg section", "isRequired": false, "isSection": true}, {"name": "trigger", "isRequired": false, "isSection": true}]

en:
Command trees are similar to regular commands with the difference being the arguments are in a tree.
By having a tree, each argument can have sub args as well as their own triggers.
See [**SkBriggy Wiki**](https://github.com/ShaneBeee/SkBriggy/wiki/Command-Tree) for more detailed info.

Command names can include namespaces, ex: `brig command tree /mycommands:somecommand`.
Defaults to `minecraft` when excluded.

**Entries/Sections**:
`executor_type` = What types of execturs can run this command (Optional, defaults to `all`).
`permission` = Just like Skript, the permission the player will require for this command.
`description` = Just like Skript, this is a string that will be used in the help command.
`usages` = This is the usage which is shown in the specific `/help <command>` page. Separate multiple usages by comma.
`aliases` = Aliases for this command.
`override` = Whether to completely wipe out other commands with the same name, such as vanilla Minecraft commands (Defaults to false).
`register arg` = Register another subcommand within this one. Supports multiple.
`trigger` = Like any other command, this is what will execute when the command is run.
